Regional Tx5d values average 0.77°C more than they would have without warming. Averaged across 1992-2013, reported as middle year.
sourcematerial: Empirical evidence from the source text states that "regional Tx5d values average 0.77°C more than they would have without warming over 1992–2013," emphasizing the impact of anthropogenic warming on temperature increase. It also notes that "the probability of extreme Tx5d values... has substantially increased, with probabilities rising by 13 p.p. across regions, on average." The same document states, "GDPpc is >7% per year lower than it would have been without anthropogenic effects on extreme heat in tropical countries," demonstrating the significant economic impact in tropical regions. These various points illustrate the outcomes related to temperature, probability of extreme heat, and GDP per capita changes due to anthropogenic climate influences.
